scholarships.name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
scholarships.provider: U.S. Department of State (administered by IIE)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Accredited U.S. colleges and universities (varies by placement)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Provides funding support, health benefit plan, and J-1 visa sponsorship for graduate study in the U.S.; specifics set by each home-country program.
benefits: Tuition and academic fees funding
Living/maintenance stipend
Round-trip international travel
Health benefit plan (accident & sickness coverage)
J-1 exchange visitor visa sponsorship
Enrichment activities and seminars
eligibility: Citizen/resident of a participating country, residing there at time of application
Not a U.S. citizen or permanent resident (dual U.S. citizens ineligible)
Hold the equivalent of a U.S. bachelor's degree with a strong academic record
Demonstrated English proficiency (e.g., TOEFL iBT 79-80+ or IELTS 6.5+)
Apply through home-country Fulbright Commission/Foundation or U.S. Embassy
Cannot pursue clinical training programs (medicine, dentistry, nursing, etc.)
documentsRequired: Standardized test scores as required by target universities (e.g., GRE/GMAT)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by country and are set by your home-country Fulbright Commission/Foundation or U.S. Embassy (typically spring-fall of the year before intake).
scholarships.durationNote: Master's 1-2 years; PhD varies by program
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Completed bachelor's degree equivalent with strong record
Valid English test scores (TOEFL/IELTS)
Defined field of study and target U.S. programs
Recommenders lined up
Your home-country Fulbright office deadline and country-specific rules

scholarships.name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers up to three years of tuition, a living and academic stipend, and travel for a full-time Stanford graduate degree.
benefits: Full tuition and associated fees (up to 3 years)
Living and academic expenses stipend
Annual travel stipend (one round-trip to/from Stanford)
Multidisciplinary leadership development program
Access to a global scholar community and Denning House
KHeystone projects, workshops, and guest-speaker events
eligibility: Citizens and residents of all countries may apply
First bachelor's degree earned in January 2020 or later (for 2027 cohort; earlier with military service)
Must apply to and enroll full-time in an eligible Stanford graduate program the same year
Eligible programs include DMA, JD, MA, MBA, MD, MFA, MPP, MS, and PhD
No age restrictions
Prior graduate degrees permitted if first bachelor's meets recency requirement
documentsRequired: Test scores (only if required by the chosen degree program)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Early October deadline; you must also meet your Stanford degree program's separate deadline.
scholarships.durationNote: Up to 3 years (length of the graduate degree)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Eligible bachelor's degree (earned January 2020 or later)
A chosen eligible Stanford graduate program
Two recommenders lined up
Resume and transcripts ready
Draft essay and short-answer responses

scholarships.name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Gates Cambridge Trust (funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers the full cost of postgraduate study at Cambridge, including fees and a maintenance allowance.
benefits: University Composition Fee (tuition) at the appropriate rate
Maintenance allowance of £22,050 for 12 months (2025-26 rate)
One economy single airfare at the start and end of the course
Inbound visa costs and the Immigration Health Surcharge
Academic development funding of £500-£2,000 for conferences and courses
Dependent children allowance
Up to 6 months maternity/paternity leave with continued maintenance
Discretionary fieldwork and hardship funding
eligibility: Citizen of any country outside the United Kingdom
Applying for a PhD, MLitt (full-time), or a one-year postgraduate course
Not eligible for undergraduate, MBA/EMBA, MFin, PGCE or certain professional courses
Demonstrated outstanding academic excellence
Commitment to improving the lives of others
Capacity for leadership
Must secure admission to the University of Cambridge
documentsRequired: Graduate application submitted via the University's Applicant Portal
Personal statement on suitability for the course
Four Gates Cambridge criteria statements (200-300 words each)
scholarships.deadlineNote: US citizens/residents round closes mid-October; international round closes December/early January depending on course.
scholarships.durationNote: 1 year Master's; up to 4 years PhD
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Chosen Cambridge course and met its entry requirements
Academic transcripts ready
Referees confirmed, including a Gates Cambridge-specific referee
Research proposal prepared (for PhD applicants)
Evidence of academic excellence, leadership and social commitment

scholarships.name: Rhodes Scholarship
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers all University of Oxford fees plus a living stipend for full-time postgraduate study.
benefits: University of Oxford course fees
Annual living stipend of £20,400 (2025-26)
Oxford application fees covered
Student visa and Immigration Health Surcharge fees
Two economy flights to and from the UK
Settling-in allowance on arrival
Access to Rhodes House and a global community of 4,500+ scholars
Academic and pastoral support, plus emergency financial assistance
eligibility: Age typically 18-23 (or under 27 with a recently completed first degree); limits vary by constituency
Completed an undergraduate/Bachelor's degree by the course start
Citizen or qualifying resident of an eligible Rhodes constituency
Strong academic record (e.g. US GPA of 3.70/4.0 preferred)
Meet the entry requirements of the chosen Oxford course
Demonstrated leadership and moral force of character
Commitment to service and the wellbeing of others
documentsRequired: Academic statement of proposed study (max ~350 words)
Between 5 and 8 references (at least 4 academic, at least 1 character)
Proof of age and citizenship (passport/birth certificate/ID)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by constituency/country; the US deadline is 1 October.
scholarships.durationNote: 2 years (Master's); DPhil funded up to 3 years
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: 5-8 referees confirmed (4 academic, 1 character)
Completed or near-complete Bachelor's degree
Chosen Oxford course meeting its entry requirements
Proof of citizenship and age
Strong record of leadership, service and extracurricular achievement

scholarships.name: Clarendon Scholarship
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers full course fees plus an annual living-costs grant for the duration of fee liability.
benefits: Full course fees paid (Home and Overseas rates)
Living-costs grant of at least £20,780/year (2025-26 rate)
Funding for the full period of fee liability of the course
Over 200 new scholarships awarded each year
Membership of the Clarendon Scholars' community and events
No separate application or application fee required
eligibility: Applying for a full-time or part-time Master's or DPhil (PhD) at Oxford
Open to all subject areas and all fields of study
No restrictions on nationality or country of residence
Awarded on outstanding academic merit and potential
Must apply by the relevant December or January course deadline
New students only (not those with deferred offers or continuing the same degree)
documentsRequired: Written work sample (where required by course)
Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable)
scholarships.deadlineNote: No separate application; apply for your course by its December or January deadline. Most offers sent by end of April.
scholarships.durationNote: Duration of fee liability (typically 1-2 years Master's, 3-4 years DPhil)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A strong academic record for a competitive Oxford graduate application
A chosen Oxford Master's or DPhil course and its deadline
Academic referees ready to submit references
A compelling statement of purpose (and research proposal for DPhil)

scholarships.name: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
scholarships.hostUniversities: Canadian universities with a Vanier CGS quota
scholarships.fundingSummary: Provides a $50,000 CAD per year stipend for three years of doctoral study.
benefits: $50,000 CAD per year for three years
Prestigious nationally recognized doctoral award
Supports study at any Canadian institution with a Vanier quota
Open to Canadian and international students
Recognizes leadership alongside academic and research excellence
eligibility: Pursuing a doctoral (PhD) degree at a Canadian institution
Must be nominated by the institution where you intend to study
Open to Canadian citizens, permanent residents, and foreign citizens
Assessed on academic excellence, research potential, and leadership
Must attain an average score of at least 3.1 in each of the three criteria
Institution must have an available nomination quota
documentsRequired: Voluntary Self-Identification Form for Indigenous applicants (optional)
scholarships.deadlineNote: National competition deadline in early November; institutional internal deadlines earlier. Note: transitioning to the Canada Graduate Scholarship - Doctoral; verify current program status.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A confirmed nomination from a Canadian institution with a Vanier quota
A strong research proposal
A personal leadership statement with demonstrated leadership
Referees ready to provide assessments
Academic transcripts

scholarships.name: Schwarzman Scholars
scholarships.fundingSummary: Fully funds a one-year Master's in Global Affairs at Tsinghua, including tuition, room, board, travel, and a stipend.
benefits: Full tuition and fees
Room and board
Round-trip travel to/from Beijing
In-country study tours
Required textbooks and course materials
Lenovo laptop
Health insurance
Monthly personal stipend
eligibility: Hold an undergraduate degree by August 1 of enrollment year
Aged 18-28 as of August 1 of enrollment year
English proficiency: TOEFL 100+, IELTS 7, Cambridge C1 185+, or Duolingo 130+ (waivable)
Demonstrated academic excellence and leadership qualities
Open to all nationalities (separate track for Chinese applicants)
No specific field of study required
documentsRequired: Official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions
scholarships.deadlineNote: Global/U.S. deadline is early-to-mid September; Chinese applicants apply in a separate window.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Completed or in-progress undergraduate degree
English test scores (if applicable)
Three recommenders lined up
Drafted leadership essay and statement of purpose
Two-page resume/CV

scholarships.name: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship (GOI-IES)
scholarships.provider: Higher Education Authority (HEA), Government of Ireland
scholarships.hostUniversities: Participating eligible Irish higher education institutions
scholarships.fundingSummary: Provides a €10,000 stipend plus a full tuition fee waiver for one year of study in Ireland.
benefits: €10,000 stipend for one year of study
Full tuition fee waiver for the scholarship year
Awarded to study at an eligible Irish institution
Prestigious government-backed award
60 scholarships available annually
eligibility: Be a national from outside the EU/EEA, Switzerland, and the UK
Study at NFQ Level 9 or 10 (Master's, postgraduate diploma, or PhD)
Hold a conditional or final offer from an eligible Irish institution at application
Demonstrate outstanding academic achievement
Not a previous GOI-IES recipient
Russian and Belarusian nationals are ineligible
documentsRequired: Two references uploaded via the online portal
Evidence of conditional or final admission offer
Personal statement / rationale for studying in Ireland
scholarships.deadlineNote: Annual deadline is typically 5pm Irish time in mid-March; outcomes announced early June.
scholarships.intake: Autumn (September) start of the academic year
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A conditional or final offer from an eligible Irish institution
Two referees ready to provide references
Academic transcripts and records
A strong rationale for studying in Ireland
